title: The Power of the Dog
logline: In 1920s Montana, a domineering rancher's psychological warfare against his brother's new wife and her sensitive son leads to a deadly game of manipulation and hidden desires.
antagonistOrConflict: The clash between Phil's toxic masculinity and the threat posed by Rose and Peter's presence in his controlled world
tone: Dark, brooding, psychologically intense with undercurrents of repressed sexuality and violence

actBreaks.description: Rose and George return to the ranch as newlyweds, fundamentally altering the power dynamic and launching Phil's campaign of psychological warfare.

validation.ruling: Scene 45 shows George and Rose as newlyweds traveling to the ranch, representing the irreversible commitment that fundamentally changes the story's direction from courtship to the deadly domestic conflict that will define the rest of the narrative.

actBreaks.description: Phil begins mentoring Peter at the haymaking camp, marking a crucial shift from antagonism to a complex, dangerous intimacy.

validation.ruling: Scene 107 at the haymaking camp shows Phil working with rawhide while Rose and Peter arrive together, marking the final act's setup where all the elements for the climactic confrontation are now in place.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a slow-burn psychological study that builds tension through intimate character moments rather than plot mechanics. The structure mirrors Phil's psychological journey from dominance to vulnerability, with each act representing a different phase of his relationship with the 'invaders' of his world. The pacing is deliberately methodical, allowing small gestures and glances to carry enormous weight. Campion uses the three-act structure not for traditional plot beats, but to chart the evolution of power dynamics between characters.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script maintains a hypnotic, deliberate pace with very short scenes (average 1.2 pages) that create a mosaic effect. Key tension builds through repetitive domestic moments and Phil's increasingly erratic behavior. The middle section slows considerably as Rose's alcoholism develops, then accelerates dramatically once Phil begins his seduction/mentorship of Peter around scene 107.

incitingIncident.description: Rose serves the Burbank outfit dinner, Phil mockingly admires the paper flowers on the table and uses one to light his cigarette, causing Rose distress.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: 17 INT/EXT - BEECH MAIN STREET/THE RED MILL/DINING - EVENING 17 17

validation.ruling: Scene 17 shatters the ordinary world when Phil encounters Rose and Peter at The Red Mill, establishing the central conflict through his immediate hostility and mockery of Peter's paper flowers, which forces all characters into irreversible confrontation.

midpoint.description: George searches for Phil in the dark barn, finding Bronco Henry's saddle missing, then discovers Phil smoking in the shadows who refuses to join dinner.

validation.ruling: Scene 79 is a dinner party aftermath, not a transformative moment. Scene 78 is the true midpoint where George discovers Phil has taken Bronco Henry's saddle and refuses to join the Governor's dinner, marking Phil's definitive shift from reactive antagonism to proactive isolation and the point of no return in the family dynamics.

climax.description: Phil works with Peter's rawhide in the barn at night, the water turning pink from his wounded hand as Peter watches mesmerized by the rope-making.

validation.ruling: Scene 126 shows aftermath, not peak confrontation. Scene 125 is the climax where Phil works with Peter's potentially contaminated rawhide in the barn at night, his wounded hand bleeding into the pink water - this is the ultimate confrontation with his fate and the moment that seals his doom.

scenes.heading: 1 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/STOCKYARDS/PADDOCKS - DAY 1 1
scenes.summary: Phil Burbank observes a curious hill formation with private amusement, then directs cowhands in selecting a big calf for roping at the ranch stockyards.

scenes.heading: 2 INT - DOWNSTAIRS BURBANK RANCH HOUSE - DAY 2 2
scenes.summary: Mrs Lewis sets breakfast for the cowhands and brothers while Phil enters and refuses to eat, walking straight upstairs.

scenes.heading: 3 INT/EXT - BURBANK RANCH/HALL/BEDROOM/BATHROOM/YARD - DAY 3 3
scenes.summary: Phil enters the bedroom he shares with George, plays his banjo skillfully, and heads toward the bathroom.

scenes.heading: 4 EXT - ROAD TO BEECH - DAWN 4 4
scenes.summary: The Burbank outfit drives cattle through the dawn, with Phil criticizing two cowhands for letting cattle stray during the drive.

scenes.heading: 5 INT - THE RED MILL/BEDROOM - DAY 5 5
scenes.summary: A pale boy with hazel eyes carefully crafts tiny paper flowers and clothing from magazine pages in his bedroom.

scenes.heading: 6 EXT - TOWN OF BEECH - DAY 6 6
scenes.summary: The barkeep walks down the main street of Beech and spots a dust cloud approaching in the distance.

scenes.heading: 7 INT/EXT - THE RED MILL - DAY 7 7
scenes.summary: Rose Gordon receives word from the barkeep that twelve men will need dinner and lodging, and she begins arranging tables.

scenes.heading: 8 INT - THE RED MILL/STAIRS/HALL/BEDROOM - DAY 8 8
scenes.summary: Rose tells her thin 17-year-old son Peter they need his room for guests, and he quickly covers up his craft project.

scenes.heading: 9 EXT/INT - THE RED MILL/CHICKEN COOP/KITCHEN/DINING - DAY 9 9
scenes.summary: Peter corners a chicken in the coop while Rose plays piano loudly to drown out the noise.

scenes.heading: 10 EXT - THE RED MILL/CHICKEN COOP - DAY 10 10
scenes.summary: Peter kills the chicken by twisting its neck, watching as the headless body convulses before going still.

scenes.heading: 11 INT/EXT - THE RED MILL/BEDROOM/GRAVEYARD - DAY 11 11
scenes.summary: Rose carries sheets upstairs and watches Peter walk with his strange gait toward a small graveyard on the hill.

scenes.heading: 12 EXT - TOWN OF BEECH/GRAVEYARD- DAY 12 12
scenes.summary: Peter kneels at his father Dr. John Gordon's grave, places paper flowers under a jar, and sees the Burbank cattle approaching town.

scenes.heading: 13 EXT - TOWN OF BEECH/ENTRANCE - DAY 13 13
scenes.summary: The lead steers spook at the town buildings while residents watch from windows and Phil watches for anyone who might spook the cattle.

scenes.heading: 14 EXT - BEECH STOCKYARDS - EVE/DAY 14 14
scenes.summary: The Burbank cattle are corralled in the stockyards while George and cowhands look down empty train tracks.

scenes.heading: 15 INT/EXT - THE SALOON/BEECH STREET - EVENING 15 15
scenes.summary: Phil waits at the saloon for George before the traditional toast, becomes miffed when George arrives and declines his drink, choosing to go to The Red Mill instead.

scenes.heading: 16 EXT - BEECH MAIN STREET - EVENING 16 16
scenes.summary: The twelve-man Burbank outfit walks down the main street toward The Red Mill.

scenes.heading: 17 INT/EXT - BEECH MAIN STREET/THE RED MILL/DINING - EVENING 17 17
scenes.summary: Rose serves the Burbank outfit dinner, Phil mockingly admires the paper flowers on the table and uses one to light his cigarette, causing Rose distress.
scenes.turningPointNote: Phil's cruel mockery of Peter's paper flowers establishes the central antagonistic dynamic and causes Rose's first emotional wound

scenes.heading: 18 INT - THE RED MILL/KITCHEN/DINING ROOM - NIGHT 18 18
scenes.summary: Rose frantically serves fried chicken dinners while Peter expressionlessly carries plates to the dining room.

scenes.heading: 19 INT - THE RED MILL/DINING ROOM - NIGHT 19 19
scenes.summary: Phil tells stories about Bronco Henry to the cowhands while using Peter's paper flower to light his cigarette.

scenes.heading: 20 INT - THE RED MILL/KITCHEN - NIGHT 20 20
scenes.summary: Peter dumps dishes in the kitchen sink, his face reddening with distress, and leaves despite Rose's concerned questions.

scenes.heading: 21 INT - THE RED MILL/DINING ROOM - NIGHT 21 21
scenes.summary: George stays behind to settle the bill while Phil leaves with the others, and George hears Rose sobbing in the kitchen.

scenes.heading: 22 INT/EXT - THE SALOON BAR - NIGHT 22 22
scenes.summary: Phil drinks at the saloon with prostitutes and cowhands, feeling strangely lonely despite the revelry around him.

scenes.heading: 23 EXT - BEECH MAIN STREET - NIGHT 23 23
scenes.summary: Phil walks from the saloon toward the inn, passing drunk cowhands trying to catch a loose horse.

scenes.heading: 24 INT - THE RED MILL/UPSTAIRS/BEDROOM - NIGHT 24 24
scenes.summary: Phil searches for George in the inn, finds him standing motionless in their room, and George confronts Phil about making Rose cry.
scenes.turningPointNote: George directly confronts Phil about his cruelty to Rose, marking the first crack in their brotherhood

scenes.heading: 25 EXT - THE WILLOWS/SACRED SPOT - DAY 25 25
scenes.summary: Phil secretly makes his way through willow trees to a hidden passage, ensuring he remains unseen from the ranch.

scenes.heading: 26 EXT - SACRED SPOT/RIVER CROOK - DAY 26 26
scenes.summary: Phil bathes naked in the river, sensually rubbing dirt and sand over his body before diving into the cold water.

scenes.heading: 27 EXT - SACRED SPOT/CUBBY - DAY 27 27
scenes.summary: Phil discovers an old childhood cubby he and George built, now overgrown, containing old magazines with Bronco Henry's name on one.

scenes.heading: 28 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/BUNKHOUSE/ROCKY OUTCROP - DAY 28 28
scenes.summary: Cowhands enjoy their day off while Phil whittles a miniature chair, remaining silent when asked about Bronco Henry's horse tricks.

scenes.heading: 29 EXT - BEECH - DAY 29 29
scenes.summary: George drives his Dodge into Beech and parks outside The Red Mill next to other out-of-town vehicles.

scenes.heading: 30 INT - THE RED MILL/KITCHEN/DINING - DAY 30 30
scenes.summary: George arrives at Rose's back door asking to see her, and she reluctantly invites him in while she works.

scenes.heading: 31 INT - THE RED MILL/KITCHEN - DAY 31 31
scenes.summary: George and Rose have an awkward but gentle conversation in her kitchen while she mixes biscuits and he reads sauce bottles.
scenes.turningPointNote: George and Rose's first intimate conversation establishes their growing connection

scenes.heading: 32 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/DINING/LOUNGE - NIGHT 32 32
scenes.summary: Phil sits alone at the long dining table in fading light, George's untouched dinner waiting at the other end.

scenes.heading: 33 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/YARD/GARAGE - NIGHT 33 33
scenes.summary: Phil walks to the garage and finds George's forgotten snow chains hanging on the labeled tool wall.

scenes.heading: 34 INT - BURBANK RANCH/BROTHERS BEDROOM - NIGHT 34 34
scenes.summary: Phil lies in bed playing banjo, hears a car, but discovers it's only drunk cowhands returning, not George.

scenes.heading: 35 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/ENTRANCE/LOUNGE - NIGHT 35 35
scenes.summary: George finally returns home and sits in his overcoat, smiling as he recalls his evening.

scenes.heading: 36 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/BROTHERS BEDROOM - NIGHT 36 36
scenes.summary: Phil pretends to wake up and questions George about his trip, learning George was speaking with Mrs Gordon.

scenes.heading: 37 INT/EXT -BURBANK RANCH/KILLING SHED/YARD - DAY 37 37
scenes.summary: Phil and another cowhand butcher a hanging carcass in the killing shed, slicing through skin and fat.

scenes.heading: 38 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/KILLING SHED/YARD - DAY 38 38
scenes.summary: Phil hangs the fresh hide with others while crows and dogs wait for scraps, becoming angry as he watches George drive away again.

scenes.heading: 39 INT/EXT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/LOUNGE - DAY 39 39
scenes.summary: Phil sits at the dining table writing a letter to his parents about George's troubling relationship, blood-spattered and unwashed.

scenes.heading: 40 EXT/INT - SALT LAKE CITY HOTEL - DAY 40 40
scenes.summary: At a grand Salt Lake City hotel, the concierge sorts mail including Phil's letter addressed to his parents.

scenes.heading: 41 INT - SALT LAKE CITY HOTEL/A CORNER SUITE - DAY 41 41
scenes.summary: Phil's elderly parents read his letter describing Rose as a 'suicide-widow' and struggle to compose an appropriate reply.

scenes.heading: 42 INT/EXT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/BACK DINING ROOM - DAY 42 42
scenes.summary: George announces his engagement to Rose at breakfast, showing off Phil's whittled gifts while Phil reacts with shock and rage.
scenes.turningPointNote: George's engagement announcement is the major turning point that shatters Phil's world

scenes.heading: 43 INT - BURBANK RANCH/BARN - DAY 43 43
scenes.summary: Phil rages in the barn, violently beating his spooked horse with a saddle blanket while cowhands retreat in fear.

scenes.heading: 44 INT/EXT - MRS MUELLER’S BOARDING HOUSE/PETER’S ROOM - DAY 44 44
scenes.summary: Rose and Peter arrive at his boarding house in Herndon where he'll attend school, with Rose now wearing her wedding outfit as Mrs Burbank.

scenes.heading: 45 EXT/INT - A ROAD ON ROLLING SNOW COVERED HILLS/DODGE - DAY 45 45
scenes.summary: George and Rose drive toward the ranch as newlyweds, with George planning a dinner party to introduce her to his parents and the Governor.

scenes.heading: 46 EXT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE - EVE 46 46
scenes.summary: The old Dodge approaches the darkened, lifeless Burbank Ranch House with its headlights cutting through the snow.

scenes.heading: 47 EXT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/STEPS - EVENING 47 47
scenes.summary: Rose waits with her luggage at the ranch house steps while George parks the car, not entering until he returns.

scenes.heading: 48 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/LOUNGE - EVENING 48 48
scenes.summary: Rose enters the cold, dimly lit ranch house where Phil stands unyielding in the middle of the icy room, ignoring her attempts at conversation.

scenes.heading: 49 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/PHIL’S BEDROOM - NIGHT 49 49
scenes.summary: Phil sits alone in his bedroom mocking Rose's voice while George's bed remains empty, then hears someone lock the bathroom door.

scenes.heading: 50 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/DOUBLE ACCESS BATHROOM - NIGHT 50 50
scenes.summary: George in pajamas gestures for Rose in her nightgown to enter the shared bathroom, establishing their new intimate arrangement.

scenes.heading: 51 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/PHIL’S BEDROOM - NIGHT 51 51
scenes.summary: Phil lies in bed listening to George and Rose murmuring softly in the bathroom before turning off his light.

scenes.heading: 52 EXT - BURBANK RANCH - NIGHT 52 52
scenes.summary: Moonlight illuminates the ranch exterior with whistling wind and clouds.

scenes.heading: 53 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/GEORGE AND ROSE’S ROOM - NIGHT 53 53
scenes.summary: George and Rose have sex in their bedroom, with George moving urgently on top of Rose who remains still and silent, both looking at each other with gratitude afterward.

scenes.heading: 54 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/PHIL’S BEDROOM - NIGHT 54 54
scenes.summary: Phil lies alone smoking, his face showing rage as he hears George and Rose whispering, then gets up from bed.

scenes.heading: 55 INT - BURBANK RANCH/BARN - NIGHT 55 55
scenes.summary: Phil goes to the barn, uncovers Bronco Henry's commemorative saddle, and works saddle oil into the leather while fighting back tears.

scenes.heading: 56 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/GEORGE AND ROSE’S ROOM - DAY 56 56
scenes.summary: George shows Rose to a rose-wallpapered ladies' sitting room, promising her a surprise before leaving her alone in the freezing room.

scenes.heading: 57 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/KITCHEN - DAY 57 57
scenes.summary: Rose enters the kitchen to help with dishes, then goes outside with Mrs Lewis and Lola to see cowhands and George struggling to bring in a grand piano.

scenes.heading: 58 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/LOUNGE/DINING - EVE 58 58
scenes.summary: At dinner, Phil sits unwashed at one end of the table while George and Rose are dressed formally, then Phil leaves to read by the fire as Rose announces she'll practice piano.

scenes.heading: 59 INT/EXT - BURBANK RANCH/ROSE & GEORGE’S ROOM/YARDS - DAY 59 59
scenes.summary: Rose uses binoculars to watch George and Phil in the yards while Lola tidies the room and asks about Hollywood stars and the Governor's dinner.

scenes.heading: 60 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/STAIRCASE - DAY 60 60
scenes.summary: Rose walks downstairs with her hair styled and makeup bright, maintaining her courage.

scenes.heading: 61 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/LOUNGE - DAY 61 61
scenes.summary: Rose sets up music on the piano stand, checks for Phil through the window, and closes the open doors to the lounge.

scenes.heading: 62 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/LOUNGE - DAY 62 62
scenes.summary: Rose practices a Strauss waltz on piano, absorbed in her progress, while Lola tries to warn her as Phil enters unseen and goes upstairs.

scenes.heading: 63 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/PHIL’S BEDROOM - DAY 63 63
scenes.summary: Phil moves from his bed to open his bedroom door with his foot.

scenes.heading: 64 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/LOUNGE - DAY 64 64
scenes.summary: Rose hears Phil's door open and his banjo playing the exact same passage she was practicing, humiliating and scaring her with his musical superiority.

scenes.heading: 65 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/SURROUNDS - DAY 65 65
scenes.summary: A disturbed Rose walks away from the ranch in her fur coat toward the rocky outcrop, surprised by scattered old bones in the winter field.

scenes.heading: 66 INT/EXT - BURBANK RANCH/BARN/GARAGE - DAY 66 66
scenes.summary: Phil works with rawhide cords in the barn while George approaches in a suit to speak about something, with Phil dismissing the cowhands and rolling a cigarette.

scenes.heading: 67 EXT - MONTANA LANDSCAPE/TRAIN - DAY 67 67
scenes.summary: A train travels through the austere winter landscape between Salt Lake City and Herndon.

scenes.heading: 68 INT - TRAIN CARRIAGE - DAY 68 68
scenes.summary: The Old Lady and Old Gent play cards in first class, arguing about Tutankhamen's curse and Lord Carnarvon's death.

scenes.heading: 69 EXT - HERNDON STATION - DAY 69 69
scenes.summary: George meets his parents at Herndon station and introduces Rose as his wife to the anxious Old Lady.

scenes.heading: 70 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/STAIRWAY/DOUBLE BEDROOM - DAY 70 70
scenes.summary: Rose leads the Old Lady to her former bedroom, insisting she stay there despite the Old Lady's protests about the cold, while the Old Gent dismisses Rose to his wife.

scenes.heading: 71 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/LOUNGE/DINING ROOM - DAY 71 71
scenes.summary: Mrs Lewis and Lola set the formal dining table with starched linen and silver while cowhands bring wood for the fire.

scenes.heading: 72 INT - BURBANK RANCH/ROSE & GEORGE’S TEMP BEDROOM - DAY 72 72
scenes.summary: Rose prepares for the evening in satin underwear, her hands trembling, as George tells her the Governor is coming despite hoping weather would cancel.

scenes.heading: 73 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/KITCHEN - DAY 73 73
scenes.summary: Mrs Lewis beats mousse in the kitchen with ice chips as she spots the Governor's car approaching through the window.

scenes.heading: 74 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/LOUNGE - DAY 74 74
scenes.summary: Mrs Lewis positions herself to watch the Governor's arrival while George and Rose walk to the front door together.

scenes.heading: 75 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/FRONT YARD - DAY 75 75
scenes.summary: Cowhands gather in their mail-order western wear as George helps the Governor's wife from the car and Rose greets the important guests.

scenes.heading: 76 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/LOUNGE - DAY 76 76
scenes.summary: Mrs Lewis backs away from the window as the Governor's party approaches the house.

scenes.heading: 77 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/LOUNGE - EVENING 77 77
scenes.summary: The Governor and his wife socialize with George and Rose, discussing their European travels and Phil's Yale education, while Rose is asked to play piano.

scenes.heading: 78 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/YARD - EVE 78 78
scenes.summary: George searches for Phil in the dark barn, finding Bronco Henry's saddle missing, then discovers Phil smoking in the shadows who refuses to join dinner.

scenes.heading: 79 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/LOUNGE/DINING ROOMS - EVE 79 79
scenes.summary: Rose serves coffee with trembling hands at the dinner party, struggling to play piano while the guests notice Phil's absence and her visible distress with the feather in her hair.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rose's public humiliation and breakdown at the Governor's dinner marks her psychological collapse and Phil's successful campaign to destroy her confidence.

scenes.heading: 80 EXT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE - NIGHT 80 80
scenes.summary: George walks the Governor and his wife to their car, offering an extra blanket, then returns to the house alone.

scenes.heading: 81 INT - BURBANK RANCH/THE SENIOR BURBANK BEDROOM - NIGHT 81 81
scenes.summary: The Old Lady and Old Gent discuss Rose's poor performance at dinner, noting how the cocktails affected her and the trembling feather in her hair.

scenes.heading: 82 EXT - TREES/RIVER - DAY 82 82
scenes.summary: Spring arrives with tender buds on river trees and a lone blossom quivering above snow melt rivers.

scenes.heading: 83 EXT - HERNDON/DODGE CAR - DAY 83 83
scenes.summary: Rose drives through Herndon with Peter, glancing anxiously at her son while his father's medical books sit in the back seat.

scenes.heading: 84 INT - HERNDON/MENSWEAR SHOP - DAY 84 84
scenes.summary: Rose helps Peter try on jeans and cowboy boots in a menswear shop while he mentions his friend and refuses Rose's suggestion to invite him to the ranch.

scenes.heading: 85 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/CORRAL - DAY 85 85
scenes.summary: Phil castrates bull calves gloveless while teaching technique to cowboys, then Rose and Peter arrive and Phil pressures George about something with his bloodied bandana.

scenes.heading: 86 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/HALLWAY/BEDROOMS - DAY 86 86
scenes.summary: George and Peter move a bookcase from Phil's room to Peter's room for his father's books, while Phil objects and Peter retreats as the brothers argue.

scenes.heading: 87 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/ROSE’S BEDROOM - DAY 87 87
scenes.summary: Rose secretly drinks from a hidden flask in her closet cupboard, then covers by changing shoes when George enters asking about Peter.

scenes.heading: 88 EXT/INT - BURBANK RANCH/YARD/BACK DINING ROOM - DAY 88 88
scenes.summary: Cowhands prepare for the cattle drive while Peter watches from the dining room, barely touching his pancakes as Lola serves him.

scenes.heading: 89 EXT - BURBANK RANCH - DAY 89 89
scenes.summary: Peter observes the cowhands' masculine ease and western gear while Phil gives jovial instructions about the cattle drive and moving Indians off the land.

scenes.heading: 90 EXT - HIGH COUNTRY - DAY 90 90
scenes.summary: The men escort cattle to high pastures through aspens and wildflowers, with Phil and others heading back down while discussing Indian arrowheads found at the summer camp.

scenes.heading: 91 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/ROSE’S BEDROOM - DAY 91 91
scenes.summary: Rose lies in her darkened bedroom drinking bourbon when Peter enters sweaty and adrenalized, carrying a sack and standing over her bed.

scenes.heading: 92 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/LOUNGE/DINING - DAY 92 92
scenes.summary: Phil carves himself thick slices of roast beef at George's empty place and eats alone in solitary ease.

scenes.heading: 93 INT - BURBANK RANCH/KITCHEN - DAY 93 93
scenes.summary: Mrs Lewis tells a story about golden hair found in a disturbed coffin while Rose smokes and drinks tea in the sunny kitchen.

scenes.heading: 94 INT - BURBANK RANCH/CORRIDOR - DAY 94 94
scenes.summary: Lola walks up the dark staircase carrying a carrot.

scenes.heading: 95 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/PETER’S BEDROOM - DAY 95 95
scenes.summary: Lola discovers Peter has dissected his rabbit, with its organs pulled out and pinned while he sketches them, shocking her.

scenes.heading: 96 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/LOUNGE/HALL - DAY 96 96
scenes.summary: Peter laces up new tennis shoes while Rose retrieves tennis racquets and a ball from the games cupboard.

scenes.heading: 97 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/YARD/DISUSED TENNIS COURT - DAY 97 97
scenes.summary: Rose and Peter attempt to play tennis on a disused court, discussing his rabbit dissection and surgical ambitions before Rose leaves with a migraine.

scenes.heading: 98 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/STAIRS/PHIL’S BEDROOM - DAY 98 98
scenes.summary: Phil places a fossil in his display case, then watches from his window as Rose retrieves a whisky bottle from a pit, whistling her Strauss waltz to torment her.

scenes.heading: 99 INT - BURBANK RANCH/LOUNGE - DAY 99 99
scenes.summary: Rose confronts Phil in the lounge asking why they can't be friends, and Phil responds by telling her she disgusts him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Phil's direct verbal assault on Rose represents the culmination of his psychological warfare and her desperate attempt to find peace with him.

scenes.heading: 100 INT - BURBANK RANCH/PETER’S BEDROOM/HALL - DAY 100 100
scenes.summary: Peter listens from his bedroom doorway to the confrontation, then withdraws disturbed and soothes himself by looking at gruesome medical illustrations.

scenes.heading: 101 EXT - FIELD/THE WILLOWS/RIVER - DAY 101 101
scenes.summary: Cowhands lead their horses down to the willows and river on a hot afternoon day off, many carrying towels and comic books.

scenes.heading: 102 EXT - RIVER/THE WILLOWS - DAY 102 102
scenes.summary: Cowhands swim and wrestle naked at the river while Phil arrives on horseback, watches the young men's bodies with arousal, then moves on to his private place in the willows.

scenes.heading: 103 EXT - THE WILLOWS - DAY 103 103
scenes.summary: Phil removes his clothes in his sacred willow spot and retrieves a red silk bandana with initials 'B H' from a tree hollow, holding it to his face and nose.

scenes.heading: 104 EXT - THE WILLOWS/SACRED SPOT - DAY 104 104
scenes.summary: Peter discovers Phil's hidden willow cubby containing a girly magazine marked 'Bronco Henry' and glimpses a naked bathing man in the distance before retreating.

scenes.heading: 105 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/BARN/PADDOCK - DAY 105 105
scenes.summary: Cowhands spread twelve canvas tents on the grass outside the barn, mending tears from the previous year.

scenes.heading: 106 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/SIDE OF BARN - DAY 106 106
scenes.summary: Phil shows seasonal worker Bill a beautifully woven rope and delicate horsehair watch straps made by Joe, refusing fifty dollars for the rope.

scenes.heading: 107 EXT - HAYMAKING CAMP - DAY 107 107
scenes.summary: At the haymaking camp, Phil sits cross-legged soaking rawhide strips in water when his horse alerts him to someone approaching the park entrance.

scenes.heading: 108 EXT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/GARDEN - DAY 108 108
scenes.summary: Rose kneels by the ranch steps attempting to make a garden.

scenes.heading: 109 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/KILLING SHED - DAY 109 109
scenes.summary: Peter digs blood-enriched soil under hanging hides, then abandons his wheelbarrow to eagerly follow Phil into the barn while Rose struggles with the heavy wheelbarrow.

scenes.heading: 110 EXT/INT - BURBANK RANCH/BARN - DAY 110 110
scenes.summary: Phil places Bronco Henry's saddle on a sawhorse and has Peter sit on it, telling him about Bronco Henry being the greatest horseman he knew and warning Peter not to let his mother make him a sissy.
scenes.turningPointNote: Phil begins actively mentoring Peter, creating the intimate bond that will drive the story's climax

scenes.heading: 112 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/CORRAL (OR PADDOCK) - DAY 112 112
scenes.summary: Peter struggles badly on horseback during a riding lesson, clutching the pommel and almost falling while cowhands laugh from the rails until Phil intervenes.

scenes.heading: 113 INT - BURBANK RANCH/BACK DINING ROOM - DAY 113 113
scenes.summary: Peter wraps pancakes and bacon into a parcel for his backpack while cowhands finish breakfast in the dining room.

scenes.heading: 114 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/DISTANT PADDOCK - DAY 114 114
scenes.summary: Peter rides alone across dangerous terrain, following a perilous cow path down into a ravine where his horse stumbles but recovers.

scenes.heading: 115 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/ROSE’S BEDROOM/YARD - DAY 115 115
scenes.summary: Rose wakes alone and watches from her window as Phil and Peter ride out together, Peter showing dog-like loyalty to Phil, which builds Rose into a painful fury.

scenes.heading: 116 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/YARD/RANCH HOUSE STEPS - DAY 116 116
scenes.summary: Rose staggers drunkenly from the house in high heels, calling after Peter who doesn't look back, while she protests to George about Peter being with Phil.

scenes.heading: 117 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/HILL AND STREAM - DAY 117 117
scenes.summary: Phil spots smoke from a stream and rides down with Peter to find a Shoshone father and son camping, with Peter's horse barging past Phil to reach the grass.

scenes.heading: 118 EXT - HAYSTACK/FIELD/POLES - DAY 118 118
scenes.summary: Phil works on a pole fence while Peter wanders looking at clouds and insects, then Phil tells Peter about Bronco Henry starting to ride late and points out a cottontail rabbit.

scenes.heading: 119 EXT - HAYSTACK - DAY 119 119
scenes.summary: Phil and Peter sit in the shade eating lunch, with Phil commenting on Peter's cowboy tan and telling him about obstacles, specifically mentioning Rose's drinking problem.

scenes.heading: 120 INT/EXT (VIEW) - BURBANK RANCH/KITCHEN/KILLING SHED - DAY 120 120
scenes.summary: Rose sits hungover at the kitchen table while Lola irons, then Rose looks out the window to see Mrs. Lewis refusing to sell cow hides to the Indian father and son.

scenes.heading: 121 INT - BURBANK RANCH/STAIRCASE/BEDROOM/BATHROOM - DAY 121 121
scenes.summary: George and a cowhand carry the drunk Rose upstairs to bed, where George discovers and pours out her hidden bourbon bottle.

scenes.heading: 122 EXT - TRAIL BACK TO BURBANK RANCH HOUSE - EVE 122 122
scenes.summary: Phil and Peter ride back toward the ranch when Phil suddenly rises in his stirrups, looking intently at the empty rail beside the kill house.

scenes.heading: 123 INT - BURBANK RANCH/STAIRS/GEORGE’S BEDROOM - DAY 123 123
scenes.summary: Mrs. Lewis tells George that Phil has returned and is looking where the hides should be, while George prepares to leave Rose's bedside.

scenes.heading: 124 EXT/INT - BURBANK RANCH/BARNYARD/BARN - DAY 124 124
scenes.summary: Phil spurs his horse into the barnyard in fury over the missing hides, roughly hanging up his saddle while Peter follows asking what's wrong.

scenes.heading: 125 EXT/INT - BURBANK RANCH/BARN SIDE/BARN - DAY 125 125
scenes.summary: Peter walks in a tight circle outside the barn, then touches Phil's arm and offers his own rawhide to finish the rope, saying Phil has been good to him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Peter's intimate gesture and offering of rawhide creates the crucial physical contact and emotional breakthrough between them

scenes.heading: 126 INT - BURBANK RANCH/BARN - NIGHT 126 126
scenes.summary: Phil works with Peter's rawhide in the barn at night, the water turning pink from his wounded hand as Peter watches mesmerized by the rope-making.

scenes.heading: 127 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/BACK DINING ROOM - DAY 127 127
scenes.summary: The cowhands eat breakfast surprised that Phil is absent for the first time, with Bobby noting Phil hasn't come down yet.

scenes.heading: 128 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/YARD - DAY 128 128
scenes.summary: Phil leans against the barn in the morning light with moisture rising from the ground, then walks back to the house like an old man.

scenes.heading: 129 INT - BURBANK RANCH/CORRIDOR/PHIL’S BEDROOM - DAY 129 129
scenes.summary: George finds Phil perspiring in bed and offers to drive him to Herndon, which Phil accepts with a nod.

scenes.heading: 130 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/LIVING ROOM/KITCHEN - DAY 130 130
scenes.summary: Phil walks through the living room in his ill-fitting town suit, causing Rose to quickly retreat to the kitchen where she pours coffee with shaking hands.

scenes.heading: 131 EXT - BURBANK RANCH/YARD/BARN - DAY 131 131
scenes.summary: Phil wanders into the barn and picks up the completed lasso rope, fingering it while George finds him there, Phil's mind fogged and unable to put something together.

scenes.heading: 132 INT - BURBANK RANCH/PETER’S BEDROOM/BURBANK DRIVE - DAY 132 132
scenes.summary: Peter sits cross-legged in his bedroom working on a realistic paper rose, then watches relieved as Phil leaves the ranch in the Dodge with George.

scenes.heading: 133 EXT - SALT LAKE CITY HOTEL - DAY 133 133
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of a large grand hotel in Salt Lake City in 1925.

scenes.heading: 134 INT - SALT LAKE CITY HOTEL/CORNER SUITE - DAY 134 134
scenes.summary: The Old Lady waters geranium pots in her hotel suite while preparing to travel, then suddenly covers her face with her hands as the Old Gent comforts her.

scenes.heading: 135 INT - SALT LAKE CITY HOTEL/A CORNER SUITE/HALL - DAY 135 135
scenes.summary: The Old Gent and Old Lady leave their hotel apartment and close the door behind them.

scenes.heading: 136 INT - SALT LAKE CITY HOTEL/LOBBY - DAY 136 136
scenes.summary: The Old Lady and Old Gent follow their luggage through the grand hotel lobby toward the revolving doors.

scenes.heading: 137 INT/EXT - HERNDON FUNERAL HOME - DAY 137 137
scenes.summary: George enters the darkened funeral home carrying Phil's suit and selects a mahogany coffin with white silk interior without turning on the lights.
scenes.turningPointNote: Phil's death is confirmed through George selecting his coffin, revealing the fatal outcome of his infection

scenes.heading: 138 INT - HERNDON FUNERAL HOME/WORKROOM - DAY 138 138
scenes.summary: Phil's dead body is prepared in the funeral home workroom, shaved and dressed with bandages covering his jaw and infected hand, then lifted into his casket.

scenes.heading: 139 INT/EXT - HERNDON HOTEL/FUNERAL RECEPTION - DAY 139 139
scenes.summary: Black cars crowd outside the hotel for Phil's funeral reception, with townspeople, ranchers, and cowhands gathering around Phil's portrait while the Old Lady warns the Old Gent to stay uninvolved.

scenes.heading: 140 EXT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE - DAY 140 140
scenes.summary: Peter plays in the ranch yard with a black and white collie dog, laughing as it barks at its reflection in the ground-level windows.

scenes.heading: 141 EXT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE - EVE 141 141
scenes.summary: Peter watches the sun slip behind the house, creating a black shadow that creeps up the hillside.

scenes.heading: 142 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/LOUNGE - EVE 142 142
scenes.summary: Peter browses books in the darkening lounge, selecting religious and academic texts, then sits cross-legged to read Psalm 22:20.

scenes.heading: 143 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/PETER’S BEDROOM - NIGHT 143 143
scenes.summary: Peter washes his hands and combs his hair, then watches from his window as George and Rose return holding hands and kiss in the moonlight.

scenes.heading: 144 INT - BURBANK RANCH HOUSE/PETER’S BEDROOM - NIGHT 144 144
scenes.summary: Peter cuts the words 'Deliver my soul from the sword, my darling from the power of the dog' from Psalm 22:20 and glues them in his scrapbook next to his father's medical book about anthrax transmission through animal hides.
scenes.turningPointNote: Peter's deliberate actions reveal he intentionally infected Phil with anthrax through the diseased rawhide, completing his calculated revenge

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
